commit.message:
feat(cli): add find command with skills.sh search API (#39)

* feat(cli): add find command with skills.sh search API

Implement 'skillsdock find' command for discovering skills from the
skills.sh ecosystem.

Features:
- Non-interactive mode: skillsdock find <query> [--json]
- Interactive mode: readline-based live search with 200ms debounce (TTY only)
- Formatted table output (NAME, SOURCE, DESCRIPTION)
- Machine-readable --json output
- Defensive API response parsing (array, { results }, { skills })
- Graceful error handling: network errors, timeouts (5s), HTTP errors
- Non-TTY without query shows usage hint

No new dependencies - uses only Node.js built-in modules (readline, fetch).

Test suite: 21 new tests covering all scenarios.

Closes #20

Co-authored-by: Albarn <lc708@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix(find): harden defensive parsing and fix interactive promise race

- Guard against non-object elements (null, primitives) in API response
  arrays by filtering with typeof check before mapping
- Validate that name/source/description are strings, not just truthy
- Fix promise race condition in cmdFindInteractive: settle() before
  rl.close() so errors propagate instead of being swallowed
- Add settled flag to prevent double-resolution
- Clean up stdin data listener on completion
- Accept options.stdin/stdout for testability of interactive mode
- Export cmdFindInteractive for direct testing

Test additions:
- Non-object element filtering (null, string, number, undefined)
- Non-string property type coercion
- Interactive mode: Enter submits, empty input skips, early close,
  debounce collapse, network error rejection

Co-authored-by: Albarn <lc708@users.noreply.github.com>

feat: UX and operations improvements (Dashboard, Review, Assets, Buckets, Jobs, Logs) (#5)

* feat: add sync scope selection (all / favorites / albums)

- ImmichClient: add list_album_assets, get_album_asset_count; extend
  list_assets to accept isFavorite and isArchived filters
- AssetSyncService: add sync_favorites, sync_album, sync_albums, and
  a shared _sync_paged helper; sync_all now delegates to _sync_paged
- jobs schema: add SyncJobRequest (scope: all|favorites|albums, album_ids)
- jobs router: accept optional SyncJobRequest body, store scope in
  params_json, and forward scope/album_ids to worker
- tasks worker: run_asset_sync now accepts scope and album_ids params
  and dispatches to the appropriate AssetSyncService method
- Frontend types: add SyncScope, SyncJobRequest, ImmichAlbum
- api.ts: startSyncJob accepts optional SyncJobRequest; getAlbums now
  typed as ImmichAlbum[]
- SyncOptionsModal: new component for choosing sync scope with an
  album picker (search, multi-select checkboxes, asset count display)
- Dashboard: Sync Assets button opens SyncOptionsModal instead of
  triggering sync directly
- Jobs page: same SyncOptionsModal integration

Co-authored-by: titatom <titatom@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: use POST /api/search/metadata for asset listing (GET /api/assets removed in newer Immich)

Newer Immich versions (v1.106+) removed GET /api/assets. The replacement
is POST /api/search/metadata (v1.106-v1.117) or POST /api/search/assets
(v1.118+). Both return { items: [...], total: N } instead of a plain array.

list_assets now:
1. Tries POST /api/search/metadata first
2. Falls back to POST /api/search/assets on 404
3. Falls back to legacy GET /api/assets on another 404
4. Handles both the new { items: [] } and legacy plain-array response shapes

This fixes the '404 Cannot GET /api/assets' error seen on current Immich
instances. All existing filters (isFavorite, isArchived, type) are forwarded
via the POST body.

Co-authored-by: titatom <titatom@users.noreply.github.com>

* feat: UX and operations improvements across all pages

Dashboard:
- Replace SyncOptionsModal popup with inline SyncPanel component
  (scope radio buttons + collapsible album picker directly on page)
- Add 'Clear finished jobs' button near recent jobs with inline confirm
- clearTerminalJobs DELETE /api/jobs endpoint for bulk clear

Review page:
- Remove Edit/Less toggle — all fields (description, tags, bucket,
  sub-album) are now always editable inline
- Sub-album field includes a free-text input + dropdown of existing
  Immich albums for quick selection
- Show metadata (camera, location, type) via a lightweight toggle

Assets page:
- Add sort bar: Date, Filename, Location, Tags, Album, Type
- Sort is client-side (current page), stable, null-safe
- Extended search to also match city and tags
- AssetOut now exposes album_ids from album_ids_json

Buckets page:
- Add explanatory intro paragraph at top of page
- Add per-bucket stats (total, approved, pending) from existing
  /api/buckets/stats endpoint
- Add Tooltip component with hover popups on Priority, Mapping Mode,
  Classification Prompt, Min Confidence fields
- Show mapping_mode explanation box inside the edit form that updates
  dynamically when mode is changed

Jobs page:
- Add Pause button for active jobs (sets status='paused' in DB)
- Add Resume button for paused jobs (re-enqueues via _resume_job_task)
- Add Delete button for terminal jobs with inline confirm
- Pause/resume/delete endpoints in backend router
- JobProgressBar now handles 'paused' status (color + label)
- SyncOptionsModal still available on Jobs page for scoped sync

Logs page:
- Add text search filter (searches action, asset_id, job_run_id,
  error_message via ilike on backend)
- Add Level filter (info / warning / error) — new level column added
  to audit_logs table via Alembic migration
- Add Source filter — new source column in audit_logs
- Expandable rows: click any row to see full details_json, error,
  all IDs
- Copy JSON button on expanded rows
- Column header: Status icon | Level | Action/Source | Asset | Job | Time

Backend:
- POST /api/jobs/{id}/pause — sets status to 'paused'
- POST /api/jobs/{id}/resume — sets status to 'queued', re-enqueues
- DELETE /api/jobs/{id} — deletes terminal jobs from DB
- DELETE /api/jobs — bulk delete all terminal jobs
- audit_logs: added level (info/warning/error) and source columns
- Alembic migration: 733fd949e72e_add_level_source_to_audit_logs
- AssetOut now includes album_ids field

commit.message:
fix(cli): add explicit 'list' subcommand to active-tasks (#1033)

* fix(cli): add explicit 'list' subcommand to active-tasks

Closes #1032

* fix: extract duplicated display logic into printActiveTaskList helper

Refactored the active-tasks command to eliminate code duplication between
the parent command and list subcommand. Both handlers now call a shared
printActiveTaskList() helper function instead of duplicating ~24 lines of
console formatting logic. This prevents future maintenance issues where
one handler could be updated without the other.

* test(cli): add wiring tests for active-tasks list subcommand

Addresses review comment: the PR fixes a Commander parsing bug but had no
automated test ensuring registerActiveTaskCommands() accepts both
'hybrid-mem active-tasks' and 'hybrid-mem active-tasks list'.

Adds two tests:
- 'accepts bare active-tasks and active-tasks list': verifies neither
  form produces the original 'too many arguments' error
- 'active-tasks list subcommand is registered': verifies the list
  subcommand is present and has the expected description

* Fix test argv format for Commander.js parseAsync with from: user

The test was using incorrect argv format with { from: 'user' }. When using
{ from: 'user' }, Commander.js treats the entire array as user-provided
arguments with no special handling of argv[0] and argv[1]. This caused
the test to pass vacuously without actually exercising the commands.

Changed from:
- ['node', 'hybrid-mem', 'active-tasks'] -> ['active-tasks']
- ['node', 'hybrid-mem', 'active-tasks', 'list'] -> ['active-tasks', 'list']

This ensures the test properly validates the active-tasks command and
its list subcommand.

Cursor/recent pr bug fixes 32c4 (#3)

* fix(scheduler): run interval recurring when lastRunAtMs is missing

Interval schedules without lastRunAtMs (e.g. legacy storage or manual
edits) never passed shouldRunRecurring and never executed. Treat missing
or zero lastRunAtMs as eligible for the first alarm tick, matching UI
and MERGE_SCHEDULED_WORKFLOW_RUNS behavior.

Add unit test for interval first-run and spacing logic.

Co-authored-by: contentrewardsai <contentrewardsai@users.noreply.github.com>

* chore: sync package-lock (jayson optional utf-8-validate)

Co-authored-by: contentrewardsai <contentrewardsai@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix(solana-wallet): return true after sendResponse when lib missing

__CFS_solana_walletRoute responded synchronously then returned false,
which can close the MV3 message port before the caller receives the
reply. Match other wallet routes and async handler branches (return true).

Co-authored-by: contentrewardsai <contentrewardsai@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix(SEND_TO_ENDPOINT): honor waitForResponse (skip body read)

sendToEndpoint and waitForHttpPoll pass waitForResponse; the handler
destructured it but never used it, so disabling "wait for response"
still downloaded and parsed the full body. When false, return status
and headers only and cancel the body stream; callers that save body
JSON already no-op without saveAsVariable.

Co-authored-by: contentrewardsai <contentrewardsai@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix(messaging): return true after synchronous sendResponse in service worker

Chrome MV3: returning false immediately after sendResponse can tear down
the message port before the reply is delivered. Align all synchronous
reply paths in onMessage / onMessageExternal with async branches (return
true), including validation errors, grant acks, perps status, recording
session guards, project handlers, Apify cancel, RUN_WORKFLOW validation,
and the unknown-type fallback.

Co-authored-by: contentrewardsai <contentrewardsai@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Radar view: new icon, remove sample data, link to scenario objects, match UI theme (#53)

- Add icons/toolbar/radar_view.svg: distinct PPI-scope SVG (circle rings +
  sweep arm + blip) used exclusively for the Radar View toolbar button.
  The existing radar.png continues to serve the Add-Radar placement action.

- myRes.qrc: register radar_view.svg.

- cvistarplanner.ui: action_RadarView now references the new SVG icon.
  The action remains the rightmost item in the toolbar (unchanged order).

- RadarManager: remove hardcoded test/sample radars (loadSampleData removed).
  Add addRadar(), removeRadar(), clearAll() so the list is driven entirely
  by objects placed/loaded on the map canvas.

- CMapCanvas: emit signalRadarObjectAdded(objectId, maxRangeKm) whenever a
  RADAR object is placed interactively or loaded from a scenario.  Emit
  signalScenarioCleared() on resetScenario() and at the start of
  loadScenarioToCanvas() so the RadarManager is kept in sync.

- CVistarPlanner: connect the two new canvas signals to
  onRadarObjectPlaced() and onScenarioObjectsCleared() slots.
  onRadarObjectPlaced() allocates a sequential integer id, maps the string
  object-id to it, and calls RadarManager::addRadar().
  onScenarioObjectsCleared() closes open PPI docks and calls clearAll().

- UI theme alignment:
  * RadarListPanel stylesheet updated from green-on-dark terminal look to
    the app's dark-grey/blue palette (#3a3a44 bg, #4a90d9 accents, #f0f0f0
    text) matching the main toolbar and status bar.
  * Active radar list items now use #7ab8f5 (app blue highlight) instead of
    #00ff88 green.
  * RadarListDock and RadarDockWidget title-bar stylesheets updated to match
    the same palette (#48485a header, #5a5a6a border, #f0f0f0 text).
  * The PPI scope interior retains its green-on-dark radar aesthetic.

Co-authored-by: Cursor Agent <cursoragent@cursor.com>
Co-authored-by: shridharbhandiwad <shridharbhandiwad@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
feat: Add Radar View module with PPI display (#52)

Adds a complete Radar View feature to the VistarPlanner application:

- RadarManager: data model holding Radar and Detection structs with
  four sample radars (Alpha Surveillance, Bravo Fire-Control,
  Charlie Coastal [inactive], Delta Tracking) and pre-populated
  detection sets for testing.

- RadarListPanel: a dockable sidebar panel listing all radars with
  name, ID and Active/Inactive status. Double-click or 'Open PPI View'
  button emits radarSelected(int) to open the PPI display.

- RadarPPIWidget: custom QPainter-based Plan Position Indicator widget
  with dark HMI styling (black background, green radar grid).
  Features: range rings with km labels, azimuth grid lines (N/NE/E…),
  detection markers (diamond shape with track-ID label), crosshair
  at radar center, compass rose, real-time hover tooltip showing
  Range / Azimuth / Elevation / Track ID, and status pill (ACTIVE /
  INACTIVE).

- RadarDockWidget: QDockWidget wrapper around RadarPPIWidget,
  dockable/floatable/closeable/resizable/minimizable. Spawns
  floating at top-right corner, offset per open instance.

- CVistarPlanner integration: new 'Radar View' toolbar action
  (action_RadarView) toggles the radar list dock. Selecting a radar
  from the list opens a floating PPI dock; multiple radars can be
  open simultaneously. Toolbar button stays checked while the list
  dock is visible.

- VistarPlanner.pro updated with all new source/header files.

- Require HMAC signature presence (fail closed when missing)
- Verify signature against x-request-id using same algo as middleware.ts
- Use timing-safe comparison to prevent timing attacks
- Prevents header-spoofing auth bypass

C3: Fix fail-open cron guards in all 4 Erie Pro cron routes
- Changed 'if (cronSecret && ...)' to 'if (!cronSecret || ...)'
- Now rejects when CRON_SECRET is unset instead of silently passing
- Affected: sla-checker, check-grace-periods, archive-stale-leads, cleanup

C5: Default-deny empty widget origins
- isAllowedWidgetOrigin() now returns false when widgetOrigins is empty
- Previously returned true for ALL origins when LEAD_OS_WIDGET_ORIGINS was unset
- Requires explicit CORS origin configuration

Audit refs: C2, C3, C5

Co-authored-by: pinohu <pinohu@users.noreply.github.com>

* security(high): timing-safe comparisons, SSRF protection, setup-admin gating

C4: Gate /api/setup-admin with ALLOW_ADMIN_SETUP env flag
- Requires ALLOW_ADMIN_SETUP=true to enable the endpoint
- Added per-IP rate limiting (5 attempts/min)
- Replaced console.error with structured logger

H1: Use timing-safe comparison for all secret/token checks
- operator-auth-core.ts: timingSafeEqual for HMAC signature verification
- middleware.ts: timingSafeEqual for cron secret comparison
- auth-middleware.ts: already fixed in C2 commit

H7: SSRF protection on all scrape endpoints
- New shared utility: src/lib/validate-url.ts
- Blocks private IPs, cloud metadata endpoints, internal hostnames
- Restricts to HTTP/HTTPS schemes, standard ports only
- Applied to: scrape/url, scrape/batch, scrape/crawl, scrape/page, scrape/competitor

Audit refs: C4, H1, H7

Co-authored-by: pinohu <pinohu@users.noreply.github.com>

* security(medium): CORS hardening, env validation, CI expansion, config fixes

H8: Remove wildcard CORS from Erie Pro lead ingestion endpoints
- leads/inbound: CORS now restricted to CORS_ALLOWED_ORIGINS env var
- embed/submit: CORS uses EMBED_ALLOWED_ORIGINS (falls back to reflect origin)
- Both endpoints now use Vary: Origin header

M1: Remove hardcoded 'unsubscribe-token-secret' fallback
- notification-hub.ts, unsubscribe/route.ts, email/send/route.ts, cron/nurture/route.ts
- Token generation returns empty string when secrets unset instead of using known fallback

M2: Fix Windows-only test script in public runtime
- Replaced 'set VAR=val&&' with Unix-compatible 'VAR=val' syntax

M3: Add CRON_SECRET to Erie Pro env validation (env.ts)
- Validates minimum 16 chars when present

M4: Add Erie Pro to CI pipeline
- typecheck-erie, test-erie, build-erie jobs
- security-audit job runs npm audit across all 3 packages

M5: Add npm audit step to CI

M6: Reduce health endpoint info exposure
- Unauthenticated: returns only { status: 'healthy'|'degraded' }
- Authenticated (Bearer CRON_SECRET): returns full diagnostics

M7: Add missing CORS methods (PUT/PATCH/DELETE) to hybrid cors.ts

L1: Add reactStrictMode: true to hybrid and public runtime Next.js configs

Also: Updated erie-pro .env.example with new env vars

Audit refs: H8, M1-M7, L1

noUncheckedIndexedAccess surfaces hundreds of pre-existing array/object
index access patterns across all packages. This is a worthwhile strictness
improvement but requires a dedicated refactoring pass to fix all call sites.
Keeping noImplicitReturns and noFallthroughCasesInSwitch which pass cleanly.
